quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
**Aptos**

relations.note: Type scale weights must come from the approved Nunito Sans weight set.
Body size and its 150% line-height minimum apply together.
Hierarchy rule builds on the distinct sizes/weights in the type scale.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos**
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

rules.intent: Preserve approved brand typefaces per medium.
rules.rule_text: Typography hierarchy is medium-dependent. Print headlines/headers use Agenda (headline.font = Agenda) with body in Nunito Sans; digital/email uses Nunito Sans (body.font = Nunito Sans) in bolder weights and Title Case for headlines. Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). Word/PPT/Excel fallback is Aptos. Email body must use the email font stack (font.email.stack = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif), with Nunito Sans loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported and graceful fallback in Outlook.

quotes: Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

rules.intent: Restrict typography to sanctioned weight sets.
rules.rule_text: Only the approved font weights may be used: Agenda in Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans in Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos in Regular, Bold.

quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
use sparingly, never for primary content
must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Enforce the approved email type scale and legibility minimums.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 (section hero headline) 28px/36px Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 (section heading) 22px/30px Nunito Sans Bold; H3 (subhead) 18px/26px Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px Nunito Sans Regular — 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ('bigger is better'); Secondary/supporting body 14px/21px Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content; Footnote/reference text within a section 12px/18px — must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it. Never set running text below 12px (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component).

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Guarantee legible line spacing, especially for light-on-dark text.
rules.rule_text: Body copy line height must be a minimum of 150% (body.line_height = 150%), a brand accessibility rule. For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further to 160% (line_height.light_on_dark.160 = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, and last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size and weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
